name: Plutarco Elías Calles
role: Founder of PNR (proto-PRI); President 1924–1928; Jefe Máximo 1928–1936
quote: The era of caudillos must give way to the era of institutions.

name: Lázaro Cárdenas
role: President 1934–1940; nationalized oil (PEMEX); transformed PNR into PRM
quote: The oil is ours. Mexico reclaims its underground wealth for its people.

name: Miguel Alemán Valdés
role: President 1946–1952; renamed party PRI; first civilian president
quote: Mexico must modernize through industrialization and private investment.

name: Adolfo López Mateos
role: President 1958–1964; nationalized electricity; expanded social programs
quote: We stand at the extreme left within the Constitution.

name: Gustavo Díaz Ordaz
role: President 1964–1970; ordered Tlatelolco massacre October 2, 1968
quote: I assume full responsibility for what happened in Tlatelolco.

name: Luis Echeverría
role: President 1970–1976; populist turn; Corpus Christi massacre 1971
quote: The system is made of steel — it bends but does not break.

name: José López Portillo
role: President 1976–1982; oil boom and debt crisis; nationalized banks 1982
quote: I will defend the peso like a dog — I will not be abandoned by it.

name: Miguel de la Madrid
role: President 1982–1988; technocratic turn; economic crisis; 1985 earthquake
quote: Moral renovation of society is the highest imperative of our time.

name: Carlos Salinas de Gortari
role: President 1988–1994; NAFTA; neoliberal reforms; disputed 1988 election
quote: NAFTA is the door to the First World for Mexico.

name: Ernesto Zedillo
role: President 1994–2000; reformed IFE; allowed Fox victory; peso crisis 1994
quote: I will be the president who gives Mexico clean and fair elections.

name: Cuauhtémoc Cárdenas
role: PRD founder; claimed presidency was stolen in 1988; first elected Mexico City head of government
quote: The people voted. We won. The results were changed by the system.

name: Porfirio Muñoz Ledo
role: PRD co-founder; former PRI senator; later joined Morena
quote: The democratic transition requires dismantling the authoritarian apparatus from within.

name: Manuel J. Clouthier
role: PAN presidential candidate 1988; businessman; galvanized anti-PRI opposition
quote: Mexico needs citizens, not subjects. We will not accept a stolen election.

name: Diego Fernández de Cevallos
role: PAN presidential candidate 1994; senator; nicknamed 'El Jefe Diego'
quote: The PRI's time is ending. Mexico deserves genuine alternation of power.

name: Vicente Fox Quesada
role: President 2000–2006 (PAN); first non-PRI president in 71 years
quote: Today, Mexico has taken a great step toward democracy. The long night of authoritarianism is over.

name: Felipe Calderón Hinojosa
role: President 2006–2012 (PAN); launched military war on drugs; narrowly disputed election
quote: I would rather have a country with security than one with impunity. The cartels will be defeated.

name: Margarita Zavala
role: PAN congresswoman; first lady 2006–2012; independent presidential candidate 2018
quote: Mexico needs a leader who is not part of the old PRI system or the new pseudo-left.

name: Enrique Peña Nieto
role: President 2012–2018 (PRI); Pacto por México; energy reform; corruption scandals
quote: The Pacto por México will take us to the reforms that the country needs and has been waiting for.

name: Andrés Manuel López Obrador
role: President 2018–2024 (Morena); 'La Cuarta Transformación'; PRD then Morena leader
quote: For the good of all, first the poor. The Fourth Transformation is underway.

name: Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo
role: President 2024–present (Morena); first female president of Mexico; former Mexico City head of government
quote: This triumph belongs to the Mexican women and men who believe in transformation.

name: Marcelo Ebrard Casaubon
role: Morena politician; former Mexico City HoG; Foreign Minister 2018–2023; 2024 Economy Minister
quote: The relationship with the United States must be one of mutual respect, not submission.

name: Xóchitl Gálvez
role: PAN/PRI/PRD opposition presidential candidate 2024; senator; indigenous entrepreneur
quote: Mexico's institutions are under attack. We must defend democracy from those who claim to represent it.

name: Beatriz Paredes Rangel
role: PRI president multiple terms; senator; governor of Tlaxcala; 2023 presidential aspirant
quote: The PRI must reinvent itself or cease to be relevant to Mexico's future.

name: Jorge Castañeda Gutman
role: Fox's Foreign Minister 2000–2003; political scientist; independent presidential candidate 2006
quote: The transition to democracy was real but incomplete — we changed who governs, not how they govern.

name: Subcomandante Marcos (Rafael Guillén)
role: EZLN spokesman; led Zapatista uprising January 1, 1994; spokesperson for indigenous rights
quote: Today we say: enough! Ya basta! We are the product of 500 years of struggle.
